I do know that the doctors in Jinja were concerned about the possibility of HIV exposure. 
I think it is safe to say that if the doctors in Uganda were concerned about HIV exposure, the doctors in the US were concerned about it too. So the kids were tested for HIV. They were also tested for TB, which is common in Uganda. 
The other tests were done because of the kids’ age. They were both under the age of 5, and they had been exposed to all kinds of diseases. 
So, I guess the answer to your question is that the kids were tested for all kinds of things because they were young and because of their exposure to disease.

Context: Last night was our Gourmet Club's monthly dinner party with a Yucatan theme. The hostess selected recipes that were all from Rick Bayless. My assigned task was to prepare two of the appetizers. One I 'll share today : Salt and Pepper Ceviche. I didn't want to take any chances, so I went all - out and bought a nice, fresh, very expensive piece of halibut from a quality seafood market. Rick Bayless gives you a few options of fish choices, so you're not stuck with halibut if it isn't your thing ( or if it's too outrageously expensive!!. 
Question: what is the best way for the food in question to be purchased? 
Salt and Pepper Ceviche
1 pound firm white fish fillets (such as halibut, cod, or sea bass ), cut into 1/2-inch cubes
1/4 cup fresh lime juice
1/4 cup fresh orange juice
1/4 cup white wine vinegar
1/2 teaspoon kosher salt
1/2 teaspoon black pepper
1/4 cup finely chopped red onion
1/4 cup finely chopped cilantro
1/4 cup finely chopped fresh jicama
1/4 cup finely chopped fresh red radish
1/4 cup finely chopped fresh green chile, such as serrano, tomatillo, or jalapeño
1/4 cup finely chopped fresh pineapple
1/2 cup finely diced avocado
In a medium bowl, combine the fish, lime juice, orange juice, vinegar, salt, and pepper. Mix well and let marinate for 1 hour, stirring once or twice. 
Drain the fish mixture in a colander and rinse under cold water. Place in a medium bowl and add the remaining ingredients. Mix well. Serve chilled.
